# Task Closeout

Use at the end of non-trivial project work.

1. Run or report the closest practical verification.
2. Inspect `git status -sb --ignored --untracked-files=all`.
3. Report changed files and why they changed.
4. Report verification commands and results.
5. Report remaining risk or skipped checks.
6. Report project-local skills used separately from Global Skill usage.
7. Report system/global resources outside Global Memory or Global Skills when used.
8. Do not rerun or relist resolved one-off literal checks as routine gates.
9. State whether new durable knowledge should become a rule, decision, memory entry, runbook, or skill.
10. State the verified claim scope from `docs/agents/verify.yaml`: static policy pack, runtime multi-agent, hard isolation, or not claimed.
11. Include the required isolation line.

Use `not claimed` when no claim scope has current evidence.
Do not claim no deduction items unless every required proof in `docs/agents/verify.yaml` is satisfied.
Do not report static policy-pack verification as runtime employee validation or hard isolation.
